title: Yellow Fever — Hemorrhagic Phase with Jaundice

patient_data.chief_complaint: High fever, jaundice, vomiting blood — returned from West Africa 5 days ago
symptoms: Initial remission on day 3, then sudden deterioration
Jaundice — deep yellow sclera and skin
Bleeding from gums and venipuncture sites
Oliguria — urine output < 200 ml in last 12 hours
Severe headache and back pain (Faget sign — bradycardia with fever)
patient_data.history: Unvaccinated traveler who spent 3 weeks in rural Guinea for mining work. No yellow fever vaccine or malaria prophylaxis. Bitten by mosquitoes frequently. No previous travel to tropical areas.

should_detect: Yellow fever with hemorrhagic manifestations — WHO-reportable emergency
Faget sign: bradycardia (HR 58) with high fever is pathognomonic
Biphasic illness: infection phase → brief remission → intoxication phase
Hepatorenal syndrome: massive transaminitis + renal failure
DIC — thrombocytopenia + prolonged INR + mucosal bleeding
Treatment is supportive — no specific antiviral. ICU admission mandatory
Strict mosquito isolation to prevent urban transmission cycle
Vaccination of contacts and outbreak investigation needed
